behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is in handoff/merge across modules (file load + env overlay + validation + error formatting). Unit tests of validator logic alone can miss wiring bugs where one source is skipped or key-path mapping is altered. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real subprocess/network/external contract or an explicit external round-trip side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader with a temp config file missing `database.host` and with relevant `TOOL_` vars unset; assert startup fails and emitted message includes exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module configuration path resolution (env handling + path builder + loader). A pure unit test of path-join logic is insufficient to catch wiring mistakes in startup loader behavior. Rejected System because the behavior does not explicitly require subprocess/real HTTP/external contract checks; local filesystem use here is module wiring, not SYSTEM TRIAD.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing a known config file; run loader in-process and assert the loaded values come from that location (not the default home config path).

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on precedence across modules (YAML parse + env extraction + merge policy). Unit tests on merge helper alone can miss startup wiring/key-normalization mismatches. Rejected System because no explicit external contract or required external side-effect readback is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Create temp config with a conflicting value, set matching `TOOL_` env var, run loader, and assert resulting effective config uses env value over file value.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction between file reading, YAML parser exception payload, and CLI error rendering. Unit tests of formatter alone can miss parser metadata propagation issues. Rejected System because behavior text does not require subprocess or external API/contract round-trip; parser+filesystem can be validated in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML to temp config file, invoke loader, and assert failure message includes parser-reported line number (e.g., `line 7`).

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires proving an externally visible side effect (directory + file creation) at startup and that execution then proceeds. This matches SYSTEM TRIAD trigger 3 (round-trip side-effect visibility). Integration with mocks can hide real filesystem/startup-order issues.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the real CLI as a subprocess with isolated `HOME`/`XDG_CONFIG_HOME` pointing to a non-existent config dir; assert process proceeds successfully, then read back filesystem to verify directory exists and defaults file was written.
